title: First Complete Reading of a Herculaneum Scroll Achieved
kicker: Tech breakthrough in digital humanities using AI and X-ray imaging
hook: For the first time, an entire ancient Herculaneum scroll was virtually unwrapped and read without damaging it, thanks to cutting-edge technology.

blocks.html: PHerc. 1667, sealed since Vesuvius’ eruption in 79 AD, has been virtually unwrapped and read from beginning to end.

blocks.text: To read one was to destroy it.

items.title: An entire Herculaneum scroll has been read for the first time

beats.heading: High-tech imaging and AI to reveal hidden text

blocks.html: The scroll was scanned with high-resolution X-rays, reconstructed digitally, and machine learning brought out faint ink traces.

blocks.text: It is the first Herculaneum papyrus to be digitally unrolled and read in full.

blocks.html: The recovered text is a philosophical treatise on ethics, likely a Stoic work attributed to Aristocreon, dating to the 2nd century BC.

blocks.text: It turns on human nature, impulse, and the moral progress of human beings.

beats.heading: Open data and scalable method for future scrolls

blocks.html: All tomographic data, reconstructions, transcriptions, and code are openly available under a Creative Commons licence.

blocks.text: Anyone can check the work, build on it, and apply it to the scrolls that remain.

sources.title: An entire Herculaneum scroll has been read for the first time
